title: Trauma Response Self-Check
description: A brief self-awareness tool to help you recognize common trauma responses in your daily life. This is NOT a diagnostic tool — it helps you understand which responses you're experiencing so you can focus your learning.
instructions: For each item below, select the answer that best describes your experience over the past month.

questions.text: How often have you had unwanted, distressing memories of a traumatic event that seemed to come out of nowhere?
How often have you had nightmares or disturbing dreams related to a traumatic experience?
How often have you felt suddenly upset or had a strong physical reaction (racing heart, sweating, shaking) when something reminded you of a traumatic event?
How much have you tried to avoid thoughts, feelings, or memories related to a traumatic experience?
How much have you avoided places, people, or situations that remind you of a traumatic experience?
How often have you felt emotionally numb, disconnected, or unable to feel positive emotions?
How often have you felt constantly on edge, easily startled, or like you had to watch out for danger?
How much difficulty have you had concentrating, sleeping, or carrying out your daily activities because of trauma-related symptoms?

bands.label: Minimal Trauma Responses
bands.description: Your responses suggest minimal trauma-related difficulties at this time. The psychoeducation in this course can help you understand trauma and build resilience.

bands.label: Mild Trauma Responses
bands.description: Your responses suggest some trauma-related symptoms. The coping strategies and grounding techniques in this course may be helpful. Continue learning at your own pace.

bands.label: Moderate Trauma Responses
bands.description: Your responses suggest moderate trauma symptoms that may be affecting your daily life. The stabilization skills in this course are a good starting point, and we recommend considering professional trauma therapy for further support.

bands.label: Significant Trauma Responses
bands.description: Your responses suggest significant trauma symptoms. Please consider reaching out to a trauma-informed therapist or counselor. The skills in this course can help alongside professional support, but they are not a substitute for therapy.

scoring.functionalImpairmentQuestion: Overall, how much are trauma-related symptoms interfering with your ability to function in daily life?

disclaimer: This is an educational self-awareness tool, NOT a clinical assessment or diagnosis. It is designed to help you recognize common trauma responses discussed in this lesson. If you are struggling with trauma symptoms, please seek support from a licensed mental health professional. If you are in crisis, call or text 988 (Suicide & Crisis Lifeline).
